ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 public-facing app exploitation: IIS / ManageEngine / Tomcat worker spawning command interpreter or recon LOLBin
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation step where an APT41 web shell or Java deserialization payload running inside an IIS w3wp.exe / Apache Tomcat / Zoho ManageEngine (Java) host process spawns cmd/PowerShell/recon LOLBins. APT41 (BARIUM / Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon) repeatedly enters via internet-facing app exploitation (Citrix CVE-2019-19781, Zoho ADSelfService Plus CVE-2021-40539 — explicitly attributed to APT41 in CISA AA21-336A, ProxyLogon, Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228) and pivots through China Chopper-style web shells before dropping CrossWalk/ShadowPad/Cobalt Strike.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe") OR Processes.parent_process_path IN ("*\\ManageEngine\\*","*\\Apache Software Foundation\\Tomcat*","*\\inetpub\\*")) AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","schtasks.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT41 web-shell post-exploit — IIS/Java/ManageEngine spawning shell or recon LOLBin
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","schtasks.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe"]);
let _AppHostProcs = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_AppHostProcs)
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\ManageEngine\", @"\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\jboss\", @"\Confluence\", @"\Atlassian\")
| where FileName in~ (_SuspiciousChildren)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentName = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildName  = FileName,
          ChildPath  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 web-shell post-exploit — IIS/Java/ManageEngine spawning shell or recon LOLBin
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","schtasks.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\w3wp.exe","\\java.exe","\\javaw.exe","\\tomcat.exe","\\tomcat9.exe","\\httpd.exe","\\nginx.exe")
   or ParentProcessName has_any (@"\ManageEngine\", @"\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\jboss\", @"\Confluence\")
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in~ (_SuspiciousChildren)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType, SubjectUserSid
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 Web Shell Post-Exploitation - App Server Spawning Shell or LOLBin
id: 5e6d6f84-3a4f-4a5b-9b6e-apt41-uc1
status: experimental
description: Detects the canonical APT41 entry chain - exploitation of an internet-facing application (Zoho ADSelfService Plus, Citrix NetScaler, ProxyLogon, Log4Shell) where an IIS/Tomcat/Java/ManageEngine worker spawns a Windows command interpreter or recon LOLBin, indicating a web shell or deserialization payload pivot.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa21-336a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
  selection_parent_path:
    ParentImage|contains:
      - '\ManageEngine\'
      - '\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat'
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\'
      - '\jboss\'
      - '\Confluence\'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
  condition: (selection_parent or selection_parent_path) and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate app-server scripts shelling out for OS-level operations (e.g. ManageEngine update tasks, Tomcat admin scripts) - tune via process command-line allowlist
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT41 is the actor most associated with weaponising recently-disclosed app-server vulns at scale - CISA AA21-336A explicitly attributes Zoho ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus exploitation (CVE-2021-40539) to APT41, and Mandiant tracked the same cluster across Citrix NetScaler (CVE-2019-19781), ProxyLogon, and Log4Shell campaigns. Their universal next step after dropping a China Chopper-style web she

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 DLL side-loading: trusted signed binary executing from user-writable path with sibling DLL load (ShadowPad / PlugX / CrossWalk loader)
ucs.description: Detects APT41's signature loader pattern — a legitimately signed binary (commonly stolen or repurposed from McAfee, Adobe, HP, Microsoft DISM, gaming vendors) executing from AppData/Temp/ProgramData and side-loading a malicious sibling DLL. This is the canonical Winnti/ShadowPad/PlugX/CrossWalk delivery used by APT41 (Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon / Blackfly / Winnti Group / Double Dragon) across the gaming-supply-chain (Winnti) and state-espionage halves of the dual-mandate cluster.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("mcoemcpy.exe","mcvsshld.exe","mcvsmap.exe","RasTls.exe","DismHost.exe","oleview.exe","iviewers.exe","AcroRd32.exe","AdobeUpdate.exe","hpqtra08.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","VsGraphicsResources.exe","GUP.exe","BDReinit.exe","avp.exe","FFFTAS.exe","Setup.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Recycle*\\*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT41 ShadowPad/PlugX/CrossWalk side-loader: trusted signed binary in user-writable path loading sibling DLL
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _UserWritable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Recycle"]);
let _SideloadVictims = dynamic(["mcoemcpy.exe","mcvsshld.exe","mcvsmap.exe","RasTls.exe","DismHost.exe","oleview.exe","iviewers.exe","AcroRd32.exe","AdobeUpdate.exe","hpqtra08.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","VsGraphicsResources.exe","GUP.exe","BDReinit.exe","avp.exe","FFFTAS.exe"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_SideloadVictims)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_UserWritable)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath              // sibling DLL — same dir as victim binary
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")              // exclude system DLL false-positive paths
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp), LoadCount = count(),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
               SignedBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
               BinaryPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
               BinarySHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
               SideLoadedDLL = FileName,
               DLLSHA256 = SHA256
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 side-loader detection via Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoad) on Windows Event channel
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _SideloadVictims = dynamic(["mcoemcpy.exe","mcvsshld.exe","mcvsmap.exe","RasTls.exe","DismHost.exe","oleview.exe","iviewers.exe","AcroRd32.exe","AdobeUpdate.exe","hpqtra08.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","VsGraphicsResources.exe","GUP.exe","BDReinit.exe","avp.exe","FFFTAS.exe"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Signature   = tostring(EventData.Signature)
| extend BinaryName = tolower(tostring(split(Image, "\\")[-1]))
| where BinaryName in~ (_SideloadVictims)
| where Image has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\")
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| extend BinaryDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
         DllDir    = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where BinaryDir =~ DllDir                                    // sibling DLL
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Signed, Signature
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 DLL Side-Loading via Trusted Signed Binary in User-Writable Path
id: 7c1a9b2e-8d3f-4f02-b9d1-apt41-uc2
status: experimental
description: Detects APT41's hallmark loader pattern - a legitimately signed binary (McAfee, Adobe, HP, DISM, gaming vendors) executing from AppData/Temp/ProgramData and side-loading a sibling DLL. This is the canonical delivery for ShadowPad, PlugX, and CrossWalk used by APT41 / Winnti / Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon across both espionage and Winnti supply-chain operations.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
  - https://www.secureworks.com/research/threat-profiles/bronze-atlas
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\mcvsshld.exe'
      - '\mcvsmap.exe'
      - '\RasTls.exe'
      - '\DismHost.exe'
      - '\oleview.exe'
      - '\iviewers.exe'
      - '\AcroRd32.exe'
      - '\AdobeUpdate.exe'
      - '\hpqtra08.exe'
      - '\wsc_proxy.exe'
      - '\VsGraphicsResources.exe'
      - '\GUP.exe'
      - '\BDReinit.exe'
      - '\avp.exe'
      - '\FFFTAS.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
  filter_system_dll:
    ImageLoaded|startswith: 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and selection_dll and not filter_system_dll
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate self-extracting installers from listed vendors that briefly run from Temp
level: high
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading via stolen / repurposed signed third-party binaries is the single most actor-distinctive technique APT41 has used for over a decade — it is the documented loading mechanism for ShadowPad (their preferred backdoor since 2017), PlugX, CrossWalk, and the Winnti core. The named victim binaries (mcoemcpy, RasTls, DismHost, hpqtra08, VsGraphicsResources, BDReinit, avp etc.) are taken di
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
